name: get_restaurants_for_keyword
description:
Main tool to do a general search for restaurants or to search restaurants based on a keyword.
If no keyword is provided, this tool returns nearby recommended restaurants.
Only get user's location using `get_saved_addresses_for_user` tool. DO NOT ASK THE USER TO INPUT THEIR LATITUDE AND LONGITUDE DIRECTLY.

Pass the keyword in the query parameter in the following formats:
- if the user is searching for "give me restaurants serving pizza", pass "pizza" as the keyword
- if the user is searching for "show me restaurants serving pizza in 5km radius", pass "pizza from restaurants under 5km" as the keyword
- if the user is searching for "show me restaurants serving pizza under 500 rupees", pass "pizza" as the keyword and apply a filter of max_price 500
- if the user is searching for "show me restaurants serving pizza with 4 star rating", pass "pizza" as the keyword and apply a filter of min_rating 4
- if the user is searching for "show me restaurants serving pizza with 4 star rating in 5km radius", pass "pizza under 5km" as the keyword and apply a filter of min_rating 4
- if the user is searching for "show me restaurants serving pizza within 50 minutes", pass "pizza from restaurants under 50 minutes" as the keyword
- if the user is searching for "show me restaurants serving pizza under 500 with 4 star rating", pass "pizza" as the keyword and apply a filter of min_rating 4 and max_price 500
- if the user is searching for "show me restaurants serving pizza under 500 and above 300 with 4.5 star rating", pass "pizza" as the keyword and apply a filter of min_rating 4.5 and min_price 300 and max_price 500
- if the user is searching for "show me restaurants serving pizza and burger", pass "pizza and burger" as the keyword
- if the user is searching for "give me pizza from dominos", pass "pizza from dominos" as the keyword
- if the user is searching for "give me {cuisine_name} serving restaurants", pass "{cuisine_name}" as the keyword, where cuisine_name can be fever food, chinese, italian, etc.
- if the user is searching for "show me pizza premium restaurants", pass "pizza" as the keyword and set the filter `is_gourmet=True`
- if the user is searching for "show me pizza gourmet restaurants", pass "pizza" as the keyword and set the filter `is_gourmet=True`
- if the user is searching for "show me new restaurants for me", pass "new" as the keyword and set the filter `new_for_you_filter=True` (New For You means new restaurants or restaurants not ordered from before)
- if the user is searching for "show me new pizza restaurants for me", pass "pizza" as the keyword and set the filter `new_for_you_filter=True`
- if the user is searching for "show me pizza restaurants with great offers near me", pass "pizza" as the keyword and set the filter `offers_tag="default"`
- if the user is searching for "show me pizza restaurants with good offers near me", pass "pizza" as the keyword and set the filter `offers_tag="default"`
- if the user is searching for "show me pizza restaurants with DOTD offers", pass "pizza" as the keyword and set the filter `offers_tag="DOTD"`
- if the user is searching for "show me pizza restaurants with GOLD offers", pass "pizza" as the keyword and set the filter `offers_tag="GOLD"`
- if the user is searching for "show me pizza restaurants with BOGO offers", pass "pizza" as the keyword and set the filter `offers_tag="BOGO"`
- if the user is searching for "show me quick delivery restaurants", pass "quick delivery" as the keyword and set the filter `near_and_fast=True` (near_and_fast means quick delivery or fast delivery)
- if the user is searching for "show me pizza restaurants with quick delivery", pass "pizza" as the keyword and set the filter `near_and_fast=True`
- If the user intent is to compare the same dish across multiple restaurants, extract the dish name and all restaurant names, make one tool call per restaurant, and in each tool call always include the dish name using the format <dish> from <restaurant_name>, never calling the tool with only the restaurant name.

address_id.description: Address ID for the search query. Only get user's location using `get_saved_addresses_for_user` tool. DO NOT ASK THE USER TO INPUT THEIR LATITUDE AND LONGITUDE DIRECTLY.

keyword.description: The keyword to search for. It can be a restaurant name, cuisine, or any other relevant term

page_size.description: Number of restaurants to retrieve per page

anyOf.description: Represents a dynamic filter for search results.

is_gourmet.description: Filter for gourmet or premium restaurants

offers_tag.description: Filter for restaurants with offers. Supported values: 'default'-> when exact intent is not specified, 'DOTD' -> Day of the Day offers, 'GOLD' -> Zomato Gold offers, 'BOGO' -> Buy One Get One

near_and_fast.description: Filter for quick delivery (near and fast restaurants)

flash_sale.description: Filter for restaurants in flash sales or 50% off deals

anyOf.description: Postback parameters for pagination and future queries

processed_chain_ids.description: List of chain IDs that have been processed. When the user's intent is to request more results, pass the previously processed chain IDs to the call. Note that if the query filters have changed, processed chain IDs should be passed empty.

search_id.description: Unique identifier for the search operation.

result_set_key.description: Key for the result set, used for pagination or further queries.

page_index.description: Index of the current page in the search results.

previous_search_params.description: Parameters from the previous search, if any.

items.description: Represents detailed information about a restaurant.

chain_id.description: Chain identifier for the restaurant, if applicable

votes.description: Number of votes the restaurant has received

distance.description: Distance to the restaurant in kilometers

eta.description: Estimated time of arrival for delivery

items.description: A partial list of recommended menu items for the restaurant
Represents a menu item for a restaurant result.

anyOf.description: Postback parameters for pagination and future queries

processed_chain_ids.description: List of chain IDs that have been processed. When the user's intent is to request more results, pass the previously processed chain IDs to the call. Note that if the query filters have changed, processed chain IDs should be passed empty.

search_id.description: Unique identifier for the search operation.

result_set_key.description: Key for the result set, used for pagination or further queries.

page_index.description: Index of the current page in the search results.

previous_search_params.description: Parameters from the previous search, if any.

relevant_dish_ids.description: List of relevant dish IDs that should ALWAYS be passed to menu to get relevant dish results.

outputSchema.description:
SearchResults contains the data for restaurants returned from search api.
It also contains postback parameters that may be relevant for future queries such as in pagination.

name: get_restaurant_menu_by_categories
description:
Retrieves menu items for a specific restaurant, filtered by categories.
Use this API to get dishes from a restaurant's menu for multiple categories.
Call this tool to get detailed information about menu items including add_on details, variant details, prices, tags, descriptions, and reviews.

Pass `menu_filter` to filter menu items based on price, rating, and flash sale availability.
- if user had initial intent of seeing items less than 300 rupees and greater than 100 rupees, set `menu_filter.min_price` to 100 and `menu_filter.max_price` to 300.
- if user had initial intent of seeing items in flash sale or 50% off deals, set `menu_filter.flash_sale` to True.

res_id.description: Restaurant ID of the restaurant, can get id from `get_restaurants_for_keyword`.

categories.description: Categories to filter menu items by. Get available categories from `get_menu_items_listing` tool.

address_id.description: Address ID of the user location, necessary to get health details of the items. Can be obtained from `get_saved_addresses_for_user` tool.

anyOf.description: Represents a menu filter for restaurant menu items.

min_price.description: Optional minimum price of menu items

max_price.description: Optional maximum price of menu items

menu_filter.description: Filter to apply on menu items

anyOf.description: Successful response containing restaurant menu data
Represents a complete restaurant menu with all items, categories, and add-ons.

menu_items.description: List of menu items available at the restaurant

item_id.description: Unique identifier for the menu item (starts with ctl_)

description.description: Description of the menu item, if available

items.description: Represents a single variant of an item in a menu.

variant_item_id.description: Unique identifier for the item (starts with pv_)

variant_id.description: Identifier for the variant itself (starts with v_)

parent_id.description: Identifier for the parent menu group this variant belongs to.

property_values.description: Dictionary of property values of an item. Each key is a property name, such as 'size' or 'crust', and the value is the corresponding value for that property, such as 'large', 'medium' or 'thin crust', 'pan crust'.

item_metadata.description: Additional metadata about the item variant.

available_add_ons.description: List of available AddOnGroups applicable to this item variant.

discounted_price.description: Discounted price of the item variant

variant_hierarchy.description: List representing the hierarchy of variants leading to this variant.

items.description: Represents hierarchy data for a menu item.

item_id.description: Unique identifier for the hierarchy level

parent_id.description: Identifier for the parent hierarchy level

item_metadata.description: Additional metadata about the hierarchy level

parent_name.description: Name of the parent hierarchy level

variants.description: List of available variants for this item

item_tags.description: Mandatory list of tags associated with the item.

categories.description: Set of categories the item belongs to

sub_categories.description: Set of sub-categories the item belongs to

reviews.description: Number of reviews for the item

parent_id.description: Identifier for the parent menu section

available_add_ons.description: List of available AddOnGroups applicable to this item variant

anyOf.description: Represents health information for a menu item.

items.description: Represents healthy information for a menu item.

health_info.description: Healthy information about the menu item

discounted_price.description: Discounted price of the menu item

variant_id.description: Unique identifier for the item variant (starts with v_)

image_url.description: URL of the image representing the menu item

items.description:
Represents a group of add-ons for a menu item.
Each group can contain multiple add-ons and has a minimum and maximum selection limit.
The selection limits should be respected when selecting add-ons from this group.

id.description: Unique identifier for the add-on group

min_selection.description: Minimum number of add-ons that must be selected from this group

max_selection.description: Maximum number of add-ons that can be selected from this group

items.description: List of add-ons in this group
Represents an add-on for a menu item.

all_addons_data.description: List of all available add-on groups for menu items in the restaurant

categories.description: Set of available menu categories for the restaurant (e.g., 'Pizza', 'Appetizers', 'Desserts')

categories.description: A list of categories that the item belongs to

name: get_menu_items_listing
description:
Returns a mapping of menu dish names to their categories for a given restaurant.
Use this API to get the categories of a restaurant's menu first instead of fetching the entire menu to limit data.

Use this tool to:
- Search for a particular menu dish by name in a restaurant
- List all available menu dishes for a restaurant
- Find out if a restaurant has a particular menu dish
- Determine which category a menu dish belongs to
- Present a summary of all menu dishes and their categories

For detailed data about a menu dish (tags, reviews, subcategories, etc.), use `get_restaurant_menu_by_category`.

Pass `menu_filter` to filter menu items based on price, rating, and flash sale availability.
- if user had initial intent of seeing items less than 300 rupees and greater than 100 rupees, set `menu_filter.min_price` to 100 and `menu_filter.max_price` to 300.
- if user had initial intent of seeing items in flash sale or 50% off deals, set `menu_filter.flash_sale` to True.

res_id.description: Restaurant ID of the restaurant, can get id from `get_restaurants_for_keyword` tool.

address_id.description: Address ID of the user location. Can be obtained from `get_saved_addresses_for_user` tool.

anyOf.description: Represents a menu filter for restaurant menu items.

min_price.description: Optional minimum price of menu items

max_price.description: Optional maximum price of menu items

menu_filter.description: Filter to apply on menu items

anyOf.description: Successful response containing restaurant menu data
Represents a complete restaurant menu with all items, categories, and add-ons.

name: get_saved_addresses_for_user
description:
Retrieves all delivery addresses that the user has previously saved in their Zomato profile.
User must be authenticated to access their addresses.

anyOf.description: Successful response containing user's saved addresses

addresses.description: List of saved precise locations for the user

items.description: Represents a user's location with basic details.

address_id.description: Unique identifier for the user's saved address

items.description:
Represents a precise location data that is accurate upto a few meters.
Used for delivery addresses and location-based services in Zomato.

latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the location (as string for precision)

longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the location (as string for precision)

short_name.description: Brief address name for display (e.g., 'Zomato Office', 'Home')

full_name.description: Complete detailed address with floor, building, area, city details

cell_id.description: The S2 CellId string representing the location for spatial indexing and delivery zones

delivery_subzone_id.description: Zomato's internal subzone identifier for delivery logistics and serviceability

place_id.description: A unique identifier for the place in Zomato's system

address_id.description: Unique identifier for this saved address in user's profile

cell_details.description: Detailed information about the delivery cell

searched_addresses.description: List of precise locations returned from search query, if applicable

anyOf.description: Error response when addresses cannot be retrieved

error_message.description: Human-readable error message explaining what went wrong

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling

name: bind_user_number
description: bind_user_number tool is used to bind a user's phone number.

phone_number.description: The phone number to which the user should be binded. This is a mandatory field and should always be asked from user.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for the OTP request.

anyOf.description: Represents a user in the system.

is_zoman.description: Indicates if the user is a Zoman.

user.description: The user that requested the OTP.

outputSchema.description: Holds the metadata of the auth initiation request.

name: bind_user_number_verify_code
description:
bind_user_number_verify_code tool is used to verify the otp used for binding a user's phone number.

Returns:
    bool: True if the verification code was verified successfully, False otherwise.
    str: An error message if the verification failed.

auth_packet.description: The auth packet that initiated the verification process. `phone_number` field should be retrieved from the user.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for the OTP request.

anyOf.description: Represents a user in the system.

is_zoman.description: Indicates if the user is a Zoman.

user.description: The user that requested the OTP.

code.description: The verification code that the user has provided.

name: create_cart
description:
Always ask the user for the payment method. DO NOT SELECT ANY DEFAULT ON YOUR OWN.
Use this to create a cart for a user with the given items.
The menu items cannot be from multiple restaurants.
The provided menu items must only have a single variant selected,
if multiple variants are available, the user must select one variant for each item.
Always return the cart amount as well as the items in the cart as a summary after successful creation.

Note for downstream consumers (including LLMs):
    Always trust the `total_amount` field returned in the Cart object.
    Do not attempt to recalculate the total from the item prices, as the backend may apply offers, discounts, or other adjustments.
IMPORTANT: The final cart bill details should always be shown to the user before proceeding with checkout.

res_id.description: Restaurant ID for which the cart is being created.

items.description: List of items to be added to the cart.

Represents an item entry for creating a cart. This is a highly simplified version of CartItem
that is used to simplify the data that needs to be sent by an LLM to create cart.

variant_id.description: The unique variant id of the item starting with 'v_'

quantity.description: Quantity of the item to be added to the cart.

items.description: Represents an add-on entry for a cart item entry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the add-on. Starts with 'ctl_'

quantity.description: Quantity of the add-on to be added to the cart item.

add_ons.description: List of add-ons added to the item.

address_id.description: Address ID of the user location.

payment_type.description: Payment method type for the cart. Always ask the user for the payment method. DO NOT SELECT ANY DEFAULT ON YOUR OWN.

promo_code.description: Promo code name to be applied to the cart, if available.

anyOf.description: A class representing a user cart.

items.description: List of items in the cart.

Used for delivery addresses and location-based services in Zomato.

latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the location (as string for precision)

longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the location (as string for precision)

short_name.description: Brief address name for display (e.g., 'Zomato Office', 'Home')

full_name.description: Complete detailed address with floor, building, area, city details

cell_id.description: The S2 CellId string representing the location for spatial indexing and delivery zones

delivery_subzone_id.description: Zomato's internal subzone identifier for delivery logistics and serviceability

place_id.description: A unique identifier for the place in Zomato's system

address_id.description: Unique identifier for this saved address in user's profile

cell_details.description: Detailed information about the delivery cell

precise_location.description: Precise delivery location for the cart.

eta.description: Estimated time of arrival in minutes for the order associated with the cart.

distance_in_km.description: Distance in kilometers for the delivery associated with the cart.

user_message.description: IMPORTANT user message that should always be shown to the user regarding the cart.

restaurant_name.description: Name of the restaurant associated with the cart.

restaurant_rating.description: Rating of the restaurant associated with the cart.

shareable_link.description: A shareable link for the cart.

schedule_time.description: Scheduled time for the order in the format HH:MM-HH:MM.

payment_configs.description: List of payment configurations as key-value pairs.

cart.description: The cart object containing all the details of the user's cart.

success.description: Indicates whether the cart operation was successful.

message.description: A message providing additional information about the cart operation.

outputSchema.description: Represents the response returned by the cart service.

name: checkout_cart
description:
Used to checkout a cart and place an order.
The cart must be created using the `create_cart` tool.
Always ask the user for confirmation before proceeding with the checkout.

Returns:
    str: A message indicating the status of the checkout process.
    Image: A QR code for payment if the checkout was successful.

name: get_order_history
description:
This api is responsible for returning a user's order history in a location. It also supports pagination and searching order history on basis of a search query.
Do not ask the user for pagination or entering a search query on this API  unless the user explicitly asks for it.
Returns:
    OrderHistory: A list of orders that the user has placed.
    This also contains postback parameters and a has more flag that can be used to fetch more results.
    postback_params and has_more in the response will always be passed to the next request if user is asking for more results.
    this will either return OrderHistory or an error message if the location cannot be resolved.

address_id.description: The address ID to retrieve order history for.

search_query.description: Optional search query to filter order history.

request_type.description: The type of request being made, e.g., 'initial' or 'load_more'.

has_more.description: Indicates if there are more results to fetch.

start_date.description: Optional start date for filtering orders (format: YYYY-MM-DD).

end_date.description: Optional end date for filtering orders (format: YYYY-MM-DD).

anyOf.description: Successful response containing user's order history

has_more.description: Indicates if there are more order history items to fetch.

postback_params.description: Additional parameters used for pagination or filtering in subsequent requests.

items.description: Represents a single item in the order history.

is_serviceable.description: Indicates if the restaurant is serviceable.

catalogue_items.description: List of items in the order.

items.description: Represents a catalogue item in the order history.

dietary_tag.description: Dietary tag of the item (e.g., veg, non-veg, egg).

addons.description: List of addon items for the catalogue item.

items.description: Represents an addon item in the catalogue.

quantity.description: Quantity of the addon item ordered.

payment_amount.description: Total amount paid for the order.

payment_date.description: Date when the payment was made.

image_url.description: URL of the image associated with the order.

res_ddt.description: Delivery date and time for the restaurant.

anyOf.description: Error response when order history cannot be retrieved

error_message.description: Human-readable error message explaining what went wrong

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling

name: get_order_tracking_info
description:
Tool to retrieve a list of active orders that the user has placed with their current statuses.
If rider info is not present in response then it means rider is not assigned yet.

anyOf.description: Successful response containing user's order tracking information

items.description: Represents a single item in the order tracking information.

order_id.description: Unique identifier for the order tracking item.

restaurant_name.description: Name of the restaurant for the order tracking item.

message.description: Current message for the order tracking item.

is_order_paid.description: Indicates if the order is paid.

is_order_placed.description: Indicates if the order is placed.

anyOf.description: Represents rider information in the order tracking item.

chat_deeplink.description: Chat deeplink for the rider. Only used for internal handling

unread_messages_count.description: Number of unread messages from the rider.

unread_message.description: The latest unread message from the rider.

orders_delivered.description: Number of orders delivered by the rider.

rider.description: Rider information for the order tracking item.

anyOf.description: Error response when order tracking cannot be retrieved

error_message.description: Human-readable error message explaining what went wrong

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling

name: get_cart_offers
description:
Retrieves a list of available as well as applicable promos
on a cart.

Returns:
    list[Promo] | str: A list of applicable promos or an error message.

cart_id.description: The cart id of the cart for which the offers need to be fetched.

address_id.description: The address id of the delivery address.

mov.description: Minimum order value required to apply the promo

recommendation_status.description: Recommendation status of the promo if applied on a cart
